title: Stelis mucronella
taxonomy: Stelis mucronella is a species of orchid, specifically classified within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The genus Stelis is further divided into several sections, with Stelis mucronella being classified under one of these sections. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its flower and leaf structure. The species is also often grouped with other similar orchids in terms of its growth habits and geographic distribution. Further classification may involve more detailed analysis of the plant's DNA and other genetic factors.

intro: Stelis mucronella, also known as 'Mucronella Stelis', is a species of orchid native to tropical regions, particularly in Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of infections. The bioactive constituents of Stelis mucronella include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Stelis mucronella include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
